content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 16,
1994
Henry Saar
101 Camino Don Miguel
Orinda,
CA
94563—1709
Re:
Your Request for Information
Our File No.
G—94—360
Dear Mr.
Saar:
You have requested information concerning the Commission’s
investigatory procedure and administrative enforcement process.
You have specifically asked about the Commission’s stand on the
use of public monies in political campaigns and the use of
official titles by individuals in campaigns.
The Fair Political Practices Commission is empowered to
administer the provisions of the Political Reform Act
(copy
enclosed).
You will note that Section 85300 governs the use of
public monies in political campaigns and Sections 91000 et seq.
set out our enforcement authority.
Also enclosed is a copy of
Regulations 18360 and 18361.
Both regulations further define our
enforcement and administrative proceedings.
The use àf official
titles by individuals in campaigns is not an issue addressed by
the Act.
Perhaps your local civil prosecutor can assist you with
this question.
I hope this information is of assistance to you.
Pursuant to
your request, your name has been added to our press release
mailing list.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: What is the FPPC's investigatory procedure and administrative enforcement process, particularly regarding the use of public monies in political campaigns and the use of official titles by individuals in campaigns?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC administers the Political Reform Act; Section 85300 governs use of public monies in campaigns and Sections 91000 et seq. establish enforcement authority. The use of official titles in campaigns is not addressed by the Act and should be directed to local civil prosecutors.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; document is structured as a business letter response providing regulatory guidance rather than formal legal advice. Information extracted from body text and signature block.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is the FPPC's investigatory procedure and administrative enforcement process, particularly regarding the use of public monies in political campaigns and the use of official titles by individuals in campaigns?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC administers the Political Reform Act; Section 85300 governs use of public monies in campaigns and Sections 91000 et seq. establish enforcement authority. The use of official titles in campaigns is not addressed by the Act and should be directed to local civil prosecutors.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission December 16, 1994 Henry Saar 101 Camino Don Miguel Orinda, CA 94563—1709 Re: Your Request for Information Our File No. G—94—360 Dear Mr. Saar: You have requested information concerning the Commission’s investigatory procedure and administrative enforcement process. You have specifically asked about the Commission’s stand on the use of public monies in political campaigns and the use of official titles by individuals in campaigns. The Fair Political Practices Commission is empowered to administer the provisions of the Political Reform Act (copy enclosed). You will note that Section 85300 governs the use of public monies in political campaigns and Sections 91000 et seq. set out our enforcement authority. Also enclosed is a copy of Regulations 18360 and 18361. Both regulations further define our enforcement and administrative proceedings. The use àf official titles by individuals in campaigns is not an issue addressed by the Act. Perhaps your local civil prosecutor can assist you with this question. I hope this information is of assistance to you. Pursuant to your request, your name has been added to our press release mailing list. Sincerely, Steven G. Churchwell Jeanette E. Turvill Political Reform Consultant Legal Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to Henry Saar's request for information about FPPC investigatory procedures and enforcement processes, specifically regarding public monies in campaigns and official titles, directing him to relevant statutory sections and suggesting local prosecutors for the titles issue.
